2606.22430.title: Words as Difference Makers: How Large Language Models Determine Causal Structure in Text
2606.22430.paper_summary: Because large language models (LLMs) are impressively successful in predicting text, it appears that they must have access to a 'world model' representing causal and definitional structure. However, the dominant formalisms of modern causal inference -- Judea Pearl's interventionist approach and the Neyman-Rubin potential outcomes framework -- struggle to illuminate how LLMs learn causal structure. I resolve this puzzle by arguing that LLMs employ a specific inductive approach based on a difference-making logic -- sometimes called variational induction. I demonstrate how central aspects of this logic are realized during training, where LLMs require enormous amounts of text data from a wide range of contexts to identify difference- and indifference-makers within word sequences. Furthermore, I analyze specific architectural features of LLMs -- such as token embeddings and self-attention -- to determine their roles in variational induction. The difference-making logic of LLMs fundamentally parallels the experimental method, where causal relations are derived by systematically varying individual circumstances to determine their influence on a phenomenon.
2606.22430.paper_summary_zh: 因為大型語言模型（LLMs）在預測文本方面表現出色，這似乎表明它們必須擁有一個代表因果和定義結構的「世界模型」。然而，現代因果推斷的主流形式 -- 朱迪亞·珀爾的介入主義方法和奈曼-魯賓潛在結果框架 -- 在揭示 LLMs 如何學習因果結構方面面臨困難。我通過主張 LLMs 採用基於差異影響邏輯的特定歸納方法來解決這一難題 -- 有時稱為變異歸納。我展示了這一邏輯的核心方面如何在訓練過程中實現，其中 LLMs 需要來自各種背景的大量文本數據，以識別單詞序列中的差異和無差異因素。此外，我分析了 LLMs 的特定架構特徵 -- 如標記嵌入和自注意力 -- 以確定它們在變異歸納中的作用。LLMs 的差異影響邏輯根本上與實驗方法相似，因為因果關係是通過系統性地改變個別情況來確定其對現象的影響。
2606.22430.author: Wolfgang Pietsch et.al.
